blocks:
Sūrat al-Layl

Meccan, and it is 21 verses.

Sūrat wa-al-Layl

Meccan, the number of its verses is twenty-one verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا يَغْشى ** (1) By the night when it covers, It covers the sun or the day. (٢) وَالنَّهارِ إِذا تَجَلَّى ** (2) and the day when it shines! It appears with the removal of the darkness of the night. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) who said: Regarding اللَّيْلِ ** in this instance, the Night covered the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the state (دولة) that ran against him, and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) endures in their state (دولتهم) until it ends. Regarding وَالنَّهارِ إِذا تَجَلَّى , He said: The Day is al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) from us, the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them). When He rises, He will overcome the state of falsehood (دولة الباطل). He said: And the Quran sets forth parables (الأمثال) therein for the people, and He addressed His Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) with it, but as for us (نحن), none knows it other than us.

(٣) وَما خَلَقَ الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثى ** (3) and by Him Who created the male and the female! Al-Qummi said: It only means: and by Him Who created the male and the female (والذي خلق الذكر والأنثى). And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): And خَلَقَ الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثى ** (and created the male and the female) without the ما (بغير «ما»). He attributed this reading to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) also. And in al-Manaqib, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The male (الذكر) is the Commander of the Faithful, and **وَالْأُنْثى ** (and the female) is Fatimah (peace be upon them both).

(٤) إِنَّ سَعْيَكُمْ لَشَتَّى ** (4) your endeavors are indeed disparate. Indeed, your endeavors are various. Al-Qummi said: It is the answer to the oath. He said: Among you are those who strive for good, and among you are those who strive for evil. (٥) فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطى ** (5) As for him who gives obedience, وَاتَّقى ** and is wary [of Allah], of disobedience. (٦) وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنى ** (6) and confirms the best [creed], with the excellent word (الكلمة الحسنى) and the reward (المثوبة) from Allah. And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: [It means] by the Wilayah (الولاية). And likewise, He said concerning its counterpart that follows. (٧) فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرى ** (7) We shall surely ease him into the ease. Then He will grant him success until obedience becomes the easiest of matters for him. (٨) وَأَمَّا مَنْ بَخِلَ ** (8) But as for him who is stingy with what He was commanded, وَاسْتَغْنى ** and is self-sufficient, by the desires of the world, neglecting the bliss of the Hereafter (العقبى). (٩) وَكَذَّبَ بِالْحُسْنى. (9) and denies the best [creed], (١٠) فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْعُسْرى ** (10) We shall surely ease him into the difficulty. Then We shall abandon him until obedience becomes the most difficult thing for him. (١١) وَما يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مالُهُ إِذا تَرَدَّى ** (11) And his wealth will not avail him when he falls [into ruin]. when he perishes. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning a man from the Ansar (الأنصار) who had a date palm in the house of another man, and he used to enter upon him without permission. So, the man complained about this to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to the owner of the date palm: "Sell me this date palm of yours for a date palm in Paradise." He replied: "I will not do so." He said: "Sell it to me for a garden in Paradise." He replied: "I will not do so," and he departed. Then Abu al-Dahdah (أبو الدّحداح) went to him and bought it from him. He came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Messenger of Allah, take it and grant me the garden in Paradise that You offered to this man, but he did not accept it." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "You shall have gardens and gardens in Paradise." So, Allah revealed concerning this: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطى وَاتَّقى وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنى (As for him who gives and is wary [of Allah], and confirms the best [creed]), meaning Abu al-Dahdah (أبا الدحداح), up to the end of the Ayah. And it was narrated in Qurb al-Isnad from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), and in it [it is mentioned]: That Abu al-Dahdah (أبا الدحداح) bought it from him for his orchard (حائطه), and that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "Then you shall have in exchange for it a date palm in Paradise." He said: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطى (As for him who gives) means the date palm, وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنى (and confirms the best [creed]) means the promise of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And it was narrated in al-Majma' from Ibn Abbas, except that he said: A man had a date palm whose branch extended into the house of a poor man with a family. When the man came and entered the house and climbed the date palm to take dates from it, sometimes a date would fall, and the poor man's children would pick it up. The man would then climb down from the date palm until he took the date from their hands, and if he found it in the mouth (فيّ) of one of them, he would insert his finger until he extracted the date from his mouth. That man complained about this to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. Then he narrated the Hadith until he said: So Abu al-Dahdah (أبو الدحداح) bought it from him for forty date palms. He went to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Messenger of Allah, the date palm has become mine, and it is for You." So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله went to the owner of the house and said to him: "The date palm is for you and your family." Then Allah revealed the entire Surah: وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا يَغْشى (By the night when it covers). In al-Kafi and al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطى (As for him who gives) from what Allah has granted him, وَاتَّقى وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنى (and is wary [of Allah], and confirms the best [creed]), meaning that Allah gives ten for one, up to one hundred thousand and more. فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرى (We shall surely ease him into the ease), He does not desire anything good except that Allah eases it for him. وَأَمَّا مَنْ بَخِلَ (But as for him who is stingy) with what Allah has granted him, وَكَذَّبَ بِالْحُسْنى (and denies the best [creed]), meaning that Allah gives ten for one, up to one hundred thousand. فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْعُسْرى (We shall surely ease him into the difficulty), He does not desire anything evil except that He eases it for him. وَما يُغْنِي عَنْهُ مالُهُ إِذا تَرَدَّى (And his wealth will not avail him when he falls [into ruin]). He said: "By Allah, he did not fall from a mountain, nor from a wall (حائط), nor into a well, but rather he fell into the Fire of Hell (نار جهنّم)." In al-Manaqib, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطى وَاتَّقى (As for him who gives and is wary [of Allah]), He preferred [others] with his sustenance (قوته), and fasted until He fulfilled His vow, and gave His ring in charity while bowing (راكع), and preferred al-Miqdad (المقداد) over Himself regarding worldly matters. وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنى (and confirms the best [creed]), which is Paradise (الجنّة) and the reward (الثواب) from Allah. فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ (We shall surely ease him) for that, by making Him an Imam (إماماً) in goodness, an exemplar (قدوة), and the father of the Imams (peace be upon them). Allah eased Him into the ease (لِلْيُسْرى).

(١٢) إِنَّ عَلَيْنا لَلْهُدى ** (12) Indeed it is for Us to guide. Al-Qummi said: It is incumbent upon Us that We make clear (نبيّن) to them. (١٣) وَإِنَّ لَنا لَلْآخِرَةَ وَالْأُولى ** (13) And indeed to Us belong the Hereafter and the former [life]. So We grant in both abodes (الدّارين) whatever We will to whomever We will. (١٤) فَأَنْذَرْتُكُمْ ناراً تَلَظَّى ** (14) So I warn you of a fire that blazes, It flames up (تتلهّب). (١٥) لا يَصْلاها إِلَّا الْأَشْقَى. (15) which none shall enter except the most wretched, (١٦) الَّذِي كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّى. (16) who denied and turned away. In al-Majma', in the preceding narration: It means the owner of the date palm (صاحب النخلة). And Al-Qummi said: It means this one who was stingy (بخل) towards the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, he said: "In Hell (جهنّم) there is a valley (واد) in which there is a fire لا يَصْلاها إِلَّا الْأَشْقَى ** (which none shall enter except the most wretched) — [it is] so-and-so (فلان) — الَّذِي كَذَّبَ ** (who denied) the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله concerning Ali عليه السلام وَتَوَلَّى ** (and turned away) from his wilayah (ولايته)." Then he said: "The fires (النيّران) are one below the other (بعضها دون بعض). Whatever fire is in this valley (الوادي) is for the Nussab (فللنصّاب)."

(١٧) وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا الْأَتْقَى. (17) But the most Godwary will be kept away from it, (١٨) الَّذِي يُؤْتِي مالَهُ يَتَزَكَّى (18) he who gives his wealth to purify himself, Al-Qummi said: (It is) Abu al-Dahdah. And likewise in al-Majma' in the preceding narration. (١٩) وَما لِأَحَدٍ عِنْدَهُ مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ تُجْزى ** (19) and no one has any favour with him to be repaid, (meaning that) he does not intend by his giving (of wealth) to recompense it. (٢٠) إِلَّا ابْتِغاءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِ الْأَعْلى ** (20) but only seeking the Face of his Lord, the Most High. Rather, he gives it for Allah, the Exalted, purely and sincerely. (٢١) وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضى ** (21) Soon he will be satisfied. When Allah admits him into Paradise. The reward for reciting this (Surah) was previously mentioned in Surat al-Shams.
